content_en:
In the hadith there occurs,

Indeed Allah shelters His believing servant from the world just as one of you shelter your sick ones from food and drink (that would harm them). '

In the hadith concerning the saving of Misa reported by Wahb*, which is recorded by Imam Ahmad in az-Zuhd,

Allah says: ‘Indeed I drive away My friends from the delights of this world and its opulence and comfort just as the compassionate shepherd drives away his camel from the dangerous grazing lands. And indeed I make them avoid its tranquility and livelihood, just as the compassionate shepherd makes his camel to avoid the resting-places wherein it would be easy prey. This is not because I consider them to be insignificant, but so that they may complete their portion of My Kindness in safety and abundance, the delights of the world will not attract him and neither would desires overcome him.’

‘A similar Aadith to this reported by al-Bayhaqi and itis a da‘if hadith. (refer to Fayd al-Qadir).

? Wahb ibn Munabbih is a noble /abi7, but this hadith is reported from him directly to the Prophet (8) and is not authentic.

[3.2 The Reality of Passionate love (ishk)]

Therefore the only cure for the sick lies in his removing the sickness by removing this blameworthy love ftom his heart.

People are divided into two opinions concerning passionate love: One group says that if falls into the category of intentions and wishes, this being the famous opinion. Another groups says that it falls into the category of imagination and fantasies and that it is a corruption of the imagination since it causes one to depict the one who is loved in other than his true reality. This group went on to say:

And this is why Allah has not been described with passionate love (ish) and neither that He passionately loves (ya’shik) because He is far removed from this, and one cannot be praised who has these corrupt thoughts.

As for the first group, then from them are those who said:

‘He is described with passionate love (ishk) because it is a complete and perfect love and Allah loves (yubib)?

And it is reported in the narration of ‘Abdul Wahid bin Zayd that He said,

‘The servant will always continue to draw closer to me, loving Me and I loving him (A’shiguhi).’

This is the saying of some of the Sifis but the majority do not apply this word to Allah, because passionate love is a love exceeding the proper bounds, as for the Love of Allah then it has no end and cannot exceed the proper bounds. Passionate love is to be considered blameworthy without any exceptions, it is not

to be praised when it is directed towards the Creator or created because it is a love that exceeds the proper bounds.

This is also true because the word ‘passionate love’ is only employed with regards to a man loving a woman or child (or vice versa), it is not employed in things such as the love of one’s family, property or status, just as it is not employed with regards to the love of the Prophets and the righteous. Commonly, you will find this word being mentioned alongside a forbidden action, such as loving the woman who is not lawful for him, or loving a child joined with the unlawful glance and touch and other such unlawful actions.

As for the love of a man for his wife or slave-girl which leads him out of the folds of justice such that he does unlawful things for her and leaves what is obligatory - as commonly happens - even to the extent that he may oppress his son born of his old wife due to this love of his new wife, or to the extent that he will do things to keep her happy that would harm his religion and worldly life. For example his singling her out for inheritance that she does not deserve, or that he gives her family authority and property that exceeds the limits set by Allah, or he goes to excesses in spending on her, or he makes unlawful things possible for her which harms his religion and worldly life. This passionate love is forbidden with regards to one who is permissible for him, so how would it be with regards for one who has passionate love for someone who is unlawful or with regards to two men? For this contains a corruption the extent of which none can assess except the Lord of the servants; it is a sickness that corrupts the religion and objectives of the one who possesses it, then it corrupts his intelligence and then his body. Allah, the Most High, says,

(3.2 The Reality of Passionate love (ishk)]

gas

cites

rete et ec serte Co Hee GB Ow

ila oy La

LP deehecette pee te. Bae yee BUTI Sep bolls

..Then do not be soft in speech, lest in whose heart is a disease should be moved with desire, but speak in an honourable manner.

[Sarah Abzab (33) : 32]

There are some whose hearts contain the disease of desire and whose perceptions ate only skin deep. When the object of the desire submits, the sickness is satiated, and this satiation strengthens the desire and pursuit of the object and hence strengthens the sickness. This is in conttast to the one whose objective is not met, for this failure results in removing the satiation that would strengthen the sickness and thereby the desire is weakened as is the love. This is because the person definitely intends that there be action accompanying his desire, for otherwise all his desire would be is just whisperings of the soul, unless there is some speech or looking accompanying this.

As for the one who is afflicted with this passionate love but holds back and is patient, then indeed Allah will tewatd him for his ¢aqwa as occurs in the hadith:

That the one who passionately loves someone yet holds back, conceals this and is patient, then dies

upon this, will be a martyr. °

‘A da'if hadith, Refer to the discussion concerning its inauthenticity in a/-Jawab al-Kafi and Rawdah al-Mubibbin of \bn al-Qayyim and Silsilah ad-Da‘ifah of alAlbani.

This hadith is known to be the report of Yahya al-Qatat from Mujahid from Ibn ‘Abbas from the Prophet (3) but it is problematic and such a hadith is not to be depended upon.

But it is known from the evidences of the Shariah that if one were to hold back from performing that which is unlawful, be it looking, speaking or acting, and he conceals this and does not articulate it so as not to fall into that which is prohibited and he is patient in his obedience to Allah and keeping away from disobedience to Allah, despite the pain that his heart feels due to passionate love, (similar to the case of the one who is patient through a calamity), then indeed this person would gain the same reward as those who have feared Allah and been patient.

Ce ere Ger 3g,

7 oF A*>Go4 7

Orin cet

Verily, he who fears Allah and is patient, then surely Allah makes not the reward of the doers of good to be lost.

[Sarah Yusuf (12) : 90]

This holds true for the disease of envy and all other sicknesses that afflict the heart. So when the soul pursues that which would anger Allah, and the person prevents himself from this, fearing Allah, then he is included in His saying,

even canes ¢ FONTMIP LON OC) But as for him who feared the standing before

His Lord, and restrained himself from impure evil desires, and lusts. Verily, Paradise will be

/3.2 The Reality of Passionate love (ishk))

his abode. [Sérah Naz‘at (79) : 40-41]

When the soul loves something, it will do all that it can to attain it, so the one who does this out of having a blameworthy love or hatred then this action of his would be sinful. For example his hating a person due to envying him and thereby harming whosoever is linked to that person - either by preventing his rights or by showing them enmity, or his doing something that is commanded by Allah but he does it due to his desires and not for the sake of Allah.

These types of sicknesses ate commonly found in the heart. The person can hate something and due to this hate, love a great many things due to mere whims and fancies. As one poet affected by this said,

For the sake of a Sudanese girl he loved Sudan to the point that he loved the black. dogs due to his love of her.

So he loved a black girl, and therefore loved all types of black even the blackness of dogs! All of this is a sickness in the heart with regards to its imagination, fantasies and desires. We ask Allah that He eliminate all of the illnesses from our hearts, and we seek refuge with Allah from evil manners, desires and sicknesses.

[3.3 The Natural Inclination of the Heart is to love of Allah]

The heart has only been created for the worship of Allah, and this is the natural disposition (f/rah) upon which Allah created His setvants as the Prophet (:g) said,

Every new-born child is born upon the natural disposition and it is his parents that make him a Jew, Christian or a Magian, as an animal produces a perfect young animal, do you see any part of its body amputated?

Then Abi Hurayrah, (radiyAl/abu ‘anhu), said, recite if you wish the saying of Allah,

oth Sass The Fitrah of Allah with which He has created mankind. No change is there in the creation of Allah. [seerah Rum (30) : 30]*

So Allah has made the natural disposition of His servants to love Him and worship Him Alone, so if the natural disposition was to be left as it is without corrupting it, then it would be cognizant of Allah, loving Him Alone; but the natural disposition does become corrupted due to the sickness of the heart - such as the parents making it a Jew or a Christian - even though this be by the Will and Predecree of Allah, just like the body is altered by amputation. But even after this it is possible for the heart to return to the natural disposition if Allah makes this

“Reported by al-Bukhati and Muslim.

(3.3 The Natural Inclination of the Heart is to love of Allah]

easy for the one who does his utmost to return it to the natural disposition.

The Messengers were sent to affirm and re-establish the natural disposition and to perfect it, not to alter it. So when the heart loves Allah Alone, making the religion sincerely for Him, it will not be tried by the love of anyone else, not to mention be tried with passionate love because were it to be afflicted with passionate love then this would diminish its loving Allah alone. This is why when Yisuf was tried with this passionate love (directed to him) his love of Allah Alone, making the religion sincerely for him, did not allow him to be overcome by this, rather Allah said,
content_id:
Dalam hadis disebutkan: "Sesungguhnya Allah ﷻ melindungi hamba-Nya yang beriman dari dunia sebagaimana salah seorang dari kalian melindungi orang yang sakit dari makanan dan minuman yang berbahaya baginya."

Dalam hadis tentang keselamatan Musa yang diriwayatkan oleh Wahb, yang dicatat oleh Imam Ahmad dalam Az-Zuhd: "Allah ﷻ berfirman: 'Sesungguhnya Aku menjauhkan wali-wali-Ku dari kenikmatan dunia dan kemegahannya dan kenyamanannya sebagaimana gembala yang penuh kasih sayang menjauhkan untanya dari padang penggembalaan yang berbahaya. Dan sesungguhnya Aku membuat mereka menghindari ketentraman dan penghidupannya, sebagaimana gembala yang penuh kasih sayang membuat untanya menghindari tempat-tempat istirahat yang menjadikannya mangsa yang mudah. Ini bukan karena Aku menganggap mereka tidak berharga, tetapi agar mereka dapat menyelesaikan bagian mereka dari Kebaikan-Ku dengan selamat dan berlimpah; kenikmatan dunia tidak akan menarik mereka dan syahwat tidak akan mengalahkan mereka.'"

Hadis ini dha'if (lemah).

[3.2 Hakikat Cinta yang Bergelora (Isyq)]

Isyq (cinta yang bergelora) adalah cinta yang melampaui batas, yang menguasai hati secara berlebihan sehingga menjadi penyakit. Ini adalah salah satu penyakit hati yang paling berbahaya karena bisa mengarahkan seseorang kepada kemaksiatan dan kebinasaan jika cinta itu diarahkan kepada selain Allah ﷻ.

Tidak ada yang terjangkit isyq kecuali karena ada kekurangan dalam tauhid dan kecintaannya kepada Allah ﷻ. Karena jika hati benar-benar dipenuhi dengan cinta kepada Allah ﷻ, tidak ada tempat tersisa untuk cinta yang bergelora kepada selain-Nya.

Penyebab isyq antara lain: hati yang kosong dari cinta kepada Allah ﷻ dan ketakutan kepada-Nya, memandang hal-hal yang diharamkan, bergaul dengan orang-orang yang tidak bertakwa, dan banyak beristirahat.
content_ar: In the الحديث there occurs, Indeed الله shelters His believing servant from the الدنيا just as one of you shelter your sick ones from food and drink (that would harm them). ' In the الحديث فيما يتعلق بـ the saving of Misa reported by Wahb*, which is recorded by الإمام أحمد in az-الزهد, الله says: ‘Indeed I drive away My friends from the delights of this الدنيا and its opulence and comfort just as the compassionate shepherd drives away his camel from the dangerous grazing lands. And indeed I make them avoid its السكينة and livelihood, just as the compassionate shepherd makes his camel to avoid the resting-places wherein it would be easy prey. This is not لأن I consider them to be insignificant, but لذا that they may complete their portion of My اللطف in safety and abundance, the delights of the الدنيا will not attract him and neither would desires overcome him.’ ‘A similar Aadith to this reported by al-Bayhaqi and itis a da‘إذا الحديث. (refer to Fayd al-Qadir). ? Wahb ibn Munabbih is a noble /abi7, but this الحديث is reported from him directly to النبي ﷺ (8) and is not authentic. [3.2 The Reality of Passionate المحبة (ishk)] لذلك the only الدواء for the sick lies in his removing the sickness by removing this blameworthy المحبة ftom his القلب. People are divided into two opinions فيما يتعلق بـ passionate المحبة: One group says that إذا falls into the category of intentions and wishes, this being the famous opinion. Another groups says that it falls into the category of imagination and fantasies and that it is a corruption of the imagination بما أن it causes one to depict the one who is loved in other than his true reality. This group went on to say: And this is why الله has not been described with passionate المحبة (ish) and neither that He passionately loves (ya’shik) لأن He is far removed from this, and one cannot be praised who has these corrupt thoughts. أما the أولاً group, then from them are those who said: ‘He is described with passionate المحبة (ishk) لأن it is a complete and perfect المحبة and الله loves (yubib)? And أُفيد بأن in the narration of ‘Abdul Wahid bin Zayd that قال, ‘The servant will always continue to draw closer to me, loving Me and I loving him (A’shiguhi).’ This is the saying of some of the Sifis but the majority do not apply this word to الله, لأن passionate المحبة is a المحبة exceeding the proper bounds, أما the المحبة of الله then it has no end and cannot exceed the proper bounds. Passionate المحبة is to be considered blameworthy without any exceptions, it is not to be praised عندما it is directed towards the Creator or created لأن it is a المحبة that exceeds the proper bounds. This is أيضاً true لأن the word ‘passionate المحبة’ is only employed with regards to a man loving a woman or الولد (or vice versa), it is not employed in things مثل the المحبة of one’s الأسرة, property or status, just as it is not employed with regards to the المحبة of the الأنبياء and the الصالح. Commonly, you will find this word being mentioned alongside a forbidden action, مثل loving the woman who is not lawful for him, or loving a الولد joined with the unlawful glance and touch and other such unlawful actions. أما the المحبة of a man for his الزوجة or slave-girl which leads him out of the folds of العدل such that he does unlawful things for her and leaves what is obligatory - as commonly happens - even to the extent that he may oppress his son born of his old الزوجة بسبب this المحبة of his new الزوجة, or to the extent that he will do things to keep her happy that would harm his religion and worldly الحياة. على سبيل المثال his singling her out for inheritance that she does not deserve, or that he gives her الأسرة authority and property that exceeds the limits set by الله, or he goes to excesses in spending on her, or he makes unlawful things possible for her which harms his religion and worldly الحياة. This passionate المحبة is forbidden with regards to one who is permissible for him, لذا how would it be with regards for one who has passionate المحبة for someone who is unlawful or with regards to two men? For this contains a corruption the extent of which none can assess except the Lord of the servants; it is a sickness that corrupts the religion and objectives of the one who possesses it, then it corrupts his intelligence and then his الجسد. الله, the Most High, says, (3.2 The Reality of Passionate المحبة (ishk)] gas cites rete et ec serte Co Hee GB Ow ila oy La LP deehecette pee te. Bae yee BUTI Sep bolls ..Then do not be soft in speech, lest in whose القلب is a المرض should be moved with desire, but speak in an honourable manner. [Sarah Abzab (33) : 32] There are some whose القلوب contain the المرض of desire and whose perceptions ate only skin deep. عندما the object of the desire submits, the sickness is satiated, and this satiation strengthens the desire and pursuit of the object and ومن ثَم strengthens the sickness. This is in conttast to the one whose objective is not met, for this الخسارة results in removing the satiation that would strengthen the sickness and thereby the desire is weakened as is the المحبة. This is لأن the person definitely intends that there be action accompanying his desire, for otherwise all his desire would be is just whisperings of the النفس, إلا إذا there is some speech or looking accompanying this. أما the one who is afflicted with this passionate المحبة but holds back and is patient, then indeed الله will tewatd him for his ¢aqwa as occurs in the الحديث: That the one who passionately loves someone yet holds back, conceals this and is patient, then dies upon this, will be a martyr. ° ‘A ضعيف الحديث, Refer to the discussion فيما يتعلق بـ its inauthenticity in a/-Jawab al-Kafi and Rawdah al-Mubibbin of \bn al-Qayyim and Silsilah ad-Da‘ifah of alAlbani. This الحديث is known to be the report of Yahya al-Qatat from Mujahid from Ibn ‘Abbas from النبي ﷺ (3) but it is problematic and such a الحديث is not to be depended upon. But it is known from the evidences of the Shariah that إذا one were to hold back from performing that which is unlawful, be it looking, speaking or acting, and he conceals this and does not articulate it لذا as not to fall into that which is prohibited and he is patient in his obedience to الله and keeping away from disobedience to الله, رغم the الألم that his القلب feels بسبب passionate المحبة, (similar to the case of the one who is patient through a calamity), then indeed this person would gain the same الأجر as those who have feared الله and been patient. Ce ere Ger 3g, 7 oF A*>Go4 7 Orin cet Verily, he who fears الله and is patient, then surely الله makes not the الأجر of the doers of الخير to be lost. [Sarah يوسف ﷺ (12) : 90] This holds true for the المرض of الحسد and all other sicknesses that afflict the القلب. لذا عندما the النفس pursues that which would الغضب الله, and the person prevents himself from this, fearing الله, then he is included in His saying, even canes ¢ FONTMIP LON OC) But أما him who feared the standing قبل His Lord, and restrained himself from impure الشر desires, and lusts. Verily, الجنة will be /3.2 The Reality of Passionate المحبة (ishk)) his abode. [Sérah Naz‘at (79) : 40-41] عندما the النفس loves something, it will do all that it can to attain it, لذا the one who does this out of having a blameworthy المحبة or hatred then this action of his would be sinful. على سبيل المثال his hating a person بسبب envying him and thereby harming whosoever is linked to that person - either by preventing his الحقوق or by showing them enmity, or his doing something that is commanded by الله but he does it بسبب his desires and not for the sake of الله. These types of sicknesses ate commonly found in the القلب. The person can الكراهية something and بسبب this الكراهية, المحبة a great many things بسبب mere whims and fancies. As one poet affected by this said, For the sake of a Sudanese girl he loved Sudan to the point that he loved the black. dogs بسبب his المحبة of her. لذا he loved a black girl, and لذلك loved all types of black even the blackness of dogs! All of this is a sickness in the القلب with regards to its imagination, fantasies and desires. We ask الله that He eliminate all of the illnesses from our القلوب, and we seek refuge with الله from الشر الآداب, desires and sicknesses. [3.3 The Natural Inclination of the القلب is to المحبة of الله] The القلب has only been created for the worship of الله, and this is the natural disposition (f/rah) upon which الله created His setvants as النبي ﷺ (:g) said, Every new-born الولد is born upon the natural disposition and it is his الوالدان that make him a Jew, Christian or a Magian, as an animal produces a perfect young animal, do you see any part of its الجسد amputated? Then Abi Hurayrah, (radiyAl/abu ‘anhu), said, recite إذا you wish the saying of الله, oth Sass The Fitrah of الله with which He has created mankind. No change is there in the creation of الله. [seerah Rum (30) : 30]* لذا الله has made the natural disposition of His servants to المحبة Him and worship Him Alone, لذا إذا the natural disposition was to be left as it is without corrupting it, then it would be cognizant of الله, loving Him Alone; but the natural disposition does become corrupted بسبب the sickness of the القلب - مثل the الوالدان making it a Jew or a Christian - حتى وإن this be by the Will and Predecree of الله, just like the الجسد is altered by amputation. But even بعد this it is possible for the القلب to return to the natural disposition إذا الله makes this “Reported by al-Bukhati and مسلم. (3.3 The Natural Inclination of the القلب is to المحبة of الله] easy for the one who does his utmost to return it to the natural disposition. The الرسل were sent to affirm and re-establish the natural disposition and to perfect it, not to alter it. لذا عندما the القلب loves الله Alone, making the religion sincerely for Him, it will not be tried by the المحبة of anyone else, not to mention be tried with passionate المحبة لأن were it to be afflicted with passionate المحبة then this would diminish its loving الله alone. This is why عندما Yisuf was tried with this passionate المحبة (directed to him) his المحبة of الله Alone, making the religion sincerely for him, did not allow him to be overcome by this, rather الله said,
